title: Dactylorhiza × ruthei
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza × ruthei is a hybrid species of orchid, resulting from the cross between Dactylorhiza fuchsii and Dactylorhiza incarnata. It is a rare and endangered orchid species found in the wild. This orchid is classified under the family Orchidaceae and is native to the United Kingdom. Dactylorhiza × ruthei is often found growing in damp meadows and marshy areas. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and genetic makeup as a hybrid between two distinct species of orchids.
uses: Dactylorhiza × ruthei is a hybrid orchid species, and its medicinal uses are not well-documented. However, some species within the Dactylorhiza genus are used in traditional medicine for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. In traditional Indian medicine, Dactylorhiza species are used to treat various health conditions, including fever and skin disorders. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Dactylorhiza × ruthei for medicinal purposes.

side_effects: Dactylorhiza × ruthei is an orchiid hybrid that has been reported to have potential side effects if ingested, including gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with medications due to its unknown toxic compounds. intro: Dactylorhiza × ruthei, also known as Rutheit's orchid or hybrid orchid, is a rare and endangered hybrid species of orchid, resulting from the cross between Dactylorhiza fuchsii and Coeloglossum viride. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorhiza × ruthei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Dactylorhiza × ruthei is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues and as a general tonic to boost the immune system.
